diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with a chronic ulcer on the left second toe, which developed due to mechanical shearing from new socks. Physical examination revealed a dorsal ulcer with black eschar and edema, and there was no active drainage or probing to bone. Previous evaluations showed no cortical disruption on foot x-rays, and the patient was afebrile. The diagnosis of eschar was made based on the physical findings and the history of the ulcer.
